hero.title: Digitable.HappyCalendar
hero.description: Shared holidays, gifts, and wishlists become a private session that can be sent as one payload without accounts, servers, or databases.
demo.label: Live payload
workspace.title: Manage hash session
workspace.emptyEvents: No events in this group yet
workspace.openFamilyGroup: Family flow: create one group and one event, then share this payload and edit event details.
workspace.familyHint: For a couple or family: 1) create a «Family» group, 2) add one event, 3) click a calendar day to jump to that date, 4) add wishlist ideas and share the payload.
idea.title: One scenario for couples, families, and friends.
feature.calendar.title: Event calendar
feature.calendar.text: Couples, families, and friends choose dates, participation format, and responsibilities without accounts or servers.
feature.budget.title: Gift budgets
feature.budget.text: Budget is assigned to a specific category inside an event: birthdays, holidays, or anniversaries.
feature.wishlist.title: Post-event wishlists
feature.wishlist.text: After the event, participants update wishes: what was gifted, what still matters, and what should be archived.
feature.hash.title: You stay in control
feature.hash.text: The project stores nothing about you: calendar, events, and wishes live in your private payload. A web3-style flow without extra server trust.
hash.title: A session as a deck: copy a string, restore the state.
hash.description: Each update creates a new payload and password-bound fingerprint. Participants publish the fresh string in chat and compare versions by update date.
roadmap.title: Soon...
platform.web.title: Web is live
platform.web.text: You are already on the site: open the live payload, inspect the session model, and check the first group-chat scenario.
platform.mobile.title: Mobile app
platform.mobile.text: Planned React Native shell for users who prefer opening sessions on a phone.
platform.desktop.title: Desktop app
platform.desktop.text: Planned Electron shell for family and team planning on a larger screen.
platform.core.title: Shared core
platform.core.text: Next, one shared core will connect web, mobile, and desktop with the same session model, payload, and fingerprint.
